prompt: Build a ReClaim Lease & Recycling Operations Portal that supports end-to-end, offline recycling transactions tied to compliant lease contract execution. The interface, rendered with Thymeleaf server-side templates, guides three roles through clear, task-based screens: Regular Users request a recycling order by searching items with keyword, category, condition, and price-range filters (USD), using autocomplete and “trending searches” panels that update from local usage. Users choose pickup or drop-off, select an available appointment window in 30-minute increments, and receive immediate on-page feedback for validation errors (for example, appointments must be scheduled at least 2 hours in advance and no more than 14 days out). After completion, users can leave a post-transaction review with a 1–5 star rating, up to 1,000 characters of text, and up to 5 images (JPG/PNG, each ≤3 MB) to support trust and dispute resolution. Reviewers work from a queue to confirm incoming orders, adjust item categorization when needed, and initiate contract signing workflows tied to the transaction, while Administrators manage contract templates, clause field definitions, and display strategies that determine how seller credit score, positive rate, and recent review quality influence ranking and recommendation visibility. Contract pages provide a step-by-step flow (initiate, confirm, sign, archive) with a clear signature capture panel and a final, printable document view; users can also view contract status such as active, expiring soon, renewed, terminated, or voided with evidence attachments retained locally. The backend is implemented in Spring Boot, exposing REST-style endpoints consumed by the Thymeleaf views to keep the UI and business logic decoupled while remaining fully offline. Authentication is strictly local username-and-password with role-based access control for user/reviewer/admin; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, lockout occurs after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, and sessions use JWT with a 30-minute access token and a 7-day refresh token stored as an HttpOnly cookie. MySQL persists users, roles, orders, appointments, operational logs, contract templates and versions, contract instances, signature artifacts, reviews, appeals, and arbitration outcomes. Orders follow a controlled state machine (pending confirmation, accepted, completed, canceled, exception) with immutable operation logs; users may reschedule up to 2 times and may cancel without penalty until 1 hour before the appointment, after which cancellation requires reviewer approval and records a reason. Full-text search and “intelligent recommendations” are implemented using MySQL full-text indexes plus local behavior signals (recent searches, completed categories, and review sentiment proxies such as rating distributions), with deduplication by normalized item title and attribute fingerprints; all search terms and clicks are logged for admin analytics dashboards without exporting data. Contracts and evidence files are stored on local disk with checksum verification, and sensitive fields (password hashes, token secrets, signature hashes) are encrypted or salted; PII is masked in reviewer views by default and only revealed to admins with explicit access logging.
